site.title: Equiterra, Financing a Sustainable Future
site.description: Equiterra structures climate finance solutions that help governments, institutions, and project developers unlock capital, meet regulatory requirements, and deliver large-scale environmental projects.

hero.subtitle: Climate finance solutions for governments, institutions, and project developers — pairing regulatory depth with the structuring and execution that move climate capital at scale.

manifesto.h2: We structure climate finance solutions that help governments, institutions, and project developers unlock capital.
manifesto.p1: Our work combines regulatory expertise, market structuring, and execution to turn environmental value into bankable outcomes.
manifesto.p2: Not narrative. Not pledges. A financial architecture that turns sustainability into a return.

capabilities.lead: Three connected disciplines that take a climate thesis all the way to financial close.

items.p: We design strategies to unlock climate finance and navigate complex regulatory environments with technical rigor.
Access carbon pricing insights and structure market-based compliance strategies across voluntary and regulated markets.
We develop and finance large-scale environmental and infrastructure projects, connecting capital with territory.
Ensuring projects meet international sustainability standards and deliver measurable impact.
Designing financial models that unlock investment into environmental assets and infrastructure.
Navigating CBAM and global carbon frameworks to support compliance and market participation.
Developing and implementing projects from concept to execution across climate and infrastructure sectors.

cm4ge.lead: Carbon Markets for Green Economies guides environmental markets and climate finance. It ensures projects have strong integrity, aligned incentives, and long-term impact.

principles.p: Additionality and permanence built into the asset, not bolted on.
Communities and stewards participate in the value created.
Clear roles, data and accountability across every counterparty.
Structured to meet compliance, CBAM and buyer requirements.
MRV that turns outcomes into verifiable, financeable results.

founder.quote: Sustainability becomes transformational when it moves from narrative to financial architecture.
founder.name: Alexis Ludwig Leroy
founder.bio: Climate finance entrepreneur and carbon market specialist with nearly two decades of experience in environmental markets and international project development. Founder of ALLCOT and Equiterra Capital.

why.h2: We combine regulatory, financial, and market expertise to deliver scalable climate solutions.

items.p: Experience navigating carbon markets, CBAM, and complex regulatory frameworks across multiple jurisdictions.
Design financial structures that unlock investment and scale environmental projects.
Delivering projects across regions with cross-border coordination and on-the-ground implementation.

cta.p: Governments, institutions and developers partner with Equiterra to turn climate ambition into financeable reality.

hero.h1: Finance, equity and earth, engineered into one architecture.
hero.p: We are an international advisory and development firm that turns environmental value into financeable reality for governments, institutions and developers.

who.h2: Sustainability becomes transformational when it moves from narrative to financial architecture.
who.lead: Equiterra exists to close the distance between climate ambition and deployable capital, combining regulatory depth, market structuring and on-the-ground delivery.

mandate.p: We structure environmental assets so they hold up to regulation and to the market at the same time, with additionality, governance and returns aligned from day one.
li: Carbon market design, pricing and offtake

how.p: We stay in the deal from origination through monitoring, not a report and a handshake, but execution with local partners and measurable impact.

founder.quote: We don't sell pledges. We build the financial structures that make climate outcomes inevitable.
founder.bio: Climate finance entrepreneur and carbon market specialist with nearly two decades of experience. Founder of ALLCOT and Equiterra Capital, his work focuses on structuring credible environmental assets and mobilizing climate capital through carbon markets, Article 6 cooperation and nature-based solutions.

items.p: Science-based, additional and permanent, or we don't structure it.
Communities and stewards share in the value the asset creates.
Every structure is built to be funded, not to look good on a slide.

hero.h1: Strategic environmental finance, end to end.
hero.p: Three connected disciplines that take a climate thesis all the way from strategy to financial close, and delivery on the ground.

services.p: We design strategies to unlock climate finance and navigate complex regulatory environments with technical rigor, so decisions are made on architecture, not optimism.
Access carbon pricing insights and structure market-based compliance strategies across voluntary and regulated markets, with the methodology and MRV that make credits defensible.
We develop and finance large-scale environmental and infrastructure projects, connecting capital with territory, from concept to financial close and through delivery, monitoring and reporting.

process.h2: A clear path to financial close

steps.p: We map the asset, the regulation and the capital landscape.
We engineer the vehicle, the instruments and the governance.
We raise and close with the right institutional partners.
We execute, monitor and report measurable impact.

hero.h1: CBAM is coming. Be positioned, not penalised.
hero.p: The EU's carbon border tariff reshapes the economics of every carbon-intensive export. Equiterra turns that exposure into a strategy.

basics.lead: CBAM puts a carbon price on imports into the EU, starting with carbon-intensive goods, to match the cost EU producers already pay. Exporters that can measure, reduce and document emissions win; those that can't, pay.

cards.p: Iron, steel, aluminium, cement, fertilisers, hydrogen and electricity, first, with scope expanding.
Importers must report embedded emissions and, from 2026, buy CBAM certificates for them.
Accurate MRV and decarbonisation become a competitive moat, not just a cost.

timeline.p: Quarterly reporting of embedded emissions, no financial payment yet, but the data foundation is set.
Importers start purchasing CBAM certificates for embedded emissions. Real cost enters the equation.
EU ETS free allowances wind down as CBAM scales, exposure rises for unprepared exporters.
More sectors and indirect emissions are brought in. Early positioning compounds.

role.p: We turn CBAM from a compliance scramble into a strategic advantage, measuring what matters, reducing what counts, and documenting what regulators require.

faq.q: Does CBAM affect my business if I'm outside the EU?
faq.a: Yes, if you export covered goods into the EU, your importers carry the CBAM obligation, and they will pass the data and cost requirements back to you.

faq.q: What happens if I can't document my emissions?
faq.a: Default values apply, and they are deliberately conservative (high). Accurate MRV almost always lowers your effective CBAM cost.

faq.q: Can carbon credits offset CBAM exposure?
faq.a: CBAM itself is about embedded emissions, but a well-structured decarbonisation and carbon strategy reduces both your real footprint and your certificate burden. We design both together.
Now. The transitional phase is the cheap window to build the data and processes before certificates become payable.

hero.h1: Turn climate ambition into financeable reality.
hero.p: We work with the institutions that move capital and the developers that build on the ground. Tell us where you are, we'll tell you how it gets done.

items.p: National and subnational bodies designing carbon markets and mobilising climate finance.
Funds, banks and corporates seeking credible, financeable climate exposure.
Teams with environmental assets that need structuring, capital and delivery.

contact.p: Send us the shape of your mandate. We respond to qualified inquiries within two business days.
